skill.name: browser-act
skill.description: Automates browser sessions through the browser-act CLI for rendered page extraction, screenshots, forms, network capture, and session workflows.
skill.summary: Run AI-assisted browser automation through the browser-act CLI.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are true positives because the skill installs and invokes a community browser automation CLI. The network and screen-capture alerts reflect legitimate browser and verification features, but they create privacy and supply-chain risk. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithm use in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Community CLI Install and Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill authorizes browser-act Bash commands and instructs installation of an external CLI package. This is legitimate for a browser automation skill, but it creates supply-chain and command-execution risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly permits browser-act Bash commands and documents an external CLI install. The use appears functional rather than malicious, so medium severity is appropriate.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Browser Data Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill can maintain sessions, take screenshots, capture XHR or HAR responses, fill forms, and upload files. These features can expose credentials, page content, or user files if used without careful confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed browser features are explicitly present and include data capture and upload paths. The skill also documents confirmation controls, which reduces but does not remove the privacy risk.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic CLI Guidance Can Influence Agent Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to load a usage guide from the CLI and says it contains operational directives. Treating CLI output as directives can become a prompt-injection surface if the installed package or local state is compromised.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly tells the agent to consume CLI-provided operational directives. There is no confirmed malicious text, but the pattern creates a real trust-boundary concern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Homepage URL Is Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a project homepage in metadata, not an evidence of exfiltration or hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only as homepage metadata. No evidence found that the line sends data or performs a network request.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm use is present at the reported locations. The lines describe browser automation and CLI guidance, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines contain no cryptographic function names or algorithms. This is strong evidence that the static matches were unrelated text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on Bash access for browser-act commands and an external CLI install command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are explicitly documented and are required for the skill. They are not proof of malware, but they are a real execution surface.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Capture and Upload Capabilities
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents screenshots, file upload, network capture, and optional verification image upload.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The privacy-sensitive features are directly listed in the skill. The author also documents local-only handling for most data, which lowers severity.

content.value_statement: Many web tasks require JavaScript, sessions, and visual checks that simple fetch tools cannot handle. This skill routes agents through browser-act for controlled browser automation.
actual_capabilities: Installs and invokes the browser-act CLI through approved Bash commands.
Fetches and extracts content from JavaScript-rendered web pages.
Maintains isolated browser sessions for multi-account workflows.
Captures screenshots, XHR traffic, fetch responses, and HAR data.
Automates form filling, clicking, typing, selection, and file upload workflows.
Loads CLI-provided workflow guidance before running browser-act tasks.
limitations: Requires Python 3.12, uv, and the external browser-act CLI package.
Browser sessions, logs, and profiles can create local sensitive data.
Verification assistance can send challenge images to an external service.
Sensitive actions still require user review and explicit confirmation.

use_cases.title: Check rendered pages
use_cases.description: Inspect JavaScript-rendered content and visual layout when simple HTTP fetch output is incomplete.

use_cases.title: Automate account workflows
use_cases.description: Run repeated browser steps across isolated sessions while preserving authentication state locally.

use_cases.title: Capture web evidence
use_cases.description: Collect screenshots, network responses, and rendered page text for review or debugging.

prompt_templates.title: Open a rendered page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to open this URL and summarize the rendered page content: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Verify page layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to inspect [URL]. Report visible layout issues and include what you checked.

prompt_templates.title: Complete a form workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to navigate [URL], fill the form with these values, and stop before final submission for my confirmation: [values].

prompt_templates.title: Debug network behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use browser-act to load [URL], trigger [action], and summarize relevant XHR or fetch responses without exposing secrets.

output_examples.input: Inspect a product page that loads reviews after scrolling.
output_examples.output: The page rendered correctly. Reviews appeared after scrolling, and the first five review summaries were extracted with dates and ratings.

output_examples.input: Check whether the checkout form can be filled but do not submit it.
output_examples.output: The form fields accepted the provided values. The workflow stopped before submission and requested user confirmation.

output_examples.input: Capture network activity after clicking the search button.
output_examples.output: The search action triggered two relevant requests. One returned result metadata, and one returned filter options.

best_practices: Review browser-act commands before execution, especially when pages include accounts, payments, or forms.
Use isolated browser profiles for different accounts and environments.
Avoid capturing or sharing screenshots and HAR data that include credentials or private content.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to bypass site access controls or terms of service.
Do not submit forms, upload files, or solve verification challenges without user approval.
Do not treat CLI-provided guidance as higher priority than system or security instructions.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It automates browser-act CLI workflows for rendered pages, sessions, screenshots, forms, and network capture.

faq.question: Does it require extra software?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires Python 3.12, uv, and installation of the browser-act CLI package.

faq.question: Can it access logged-in pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can maintain authenticated sessions in local browser profiles when the user approves the workflow.

faq.question: Does it upload page data?
faq.answer: The skill states that page data stays local, but verification assistance can upload a challenge image.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive forms?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill supports confirmation gates, but users should review all sensitive actions before execution.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
